data.title: Bybit applies for an investment firm license in Austria
data.description:
Bybit EU Group, the European arm of Bybit, the world’s second-largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, has formally applied for an investment firm license under Austria’s implementation of the Markets in Financial Instruments Directive (MiFID II), according to the reports disclosed to Finbold on Friday, September 5. If the application is approved, the company’s local entity, Bybit X GmbH, will be able to offer regulated derivatives products such futures and options across the European Economic Area (EEA).  The application largely coincides with the official launch of Bybit’s crypto payment card in the same region, which now allows users to spend digital assets such as Bitcoin (BTC) and USD Coin (USDC) through Apple Pay, Google Pay, or a physical card. The Crypto Card you were waiting for is finally live!Enjoy 20% cashback!Request your Bybit Card today on Bybit EU!https://t
source: https://finbold.com/bybit-applies-for-an-investment-firm-license-in-austria/

data.title: Bullish Europe Secures MiCA License in Germany
data.description: According to Foresight News, CoinDesk reports that Bullish's European subsidiary has obtained a license under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation in Germany. With this MiCA license, Bullish Europe is now authorized to expand its services to any other EU country. Additionally, Bullish Europe has received this license from the German Federal Financial Supervisory Authority (BaFin), having previously acquired crypto brokerage and custody licenses from the same regulator.

data.title: Tether eyes deeper dive into gold with new $100 million investment amid market boom
data.description:
Tether, the issuer of the world’s largest stablecoin USDT, is reportedly discussing deepening its investment in gold mining companies as part of its wider expansion strategy. According to a Sept. 5 Financial Times report, the company has held discussions with mining and investment groups to explore opportunities across the entire gold supply chain
source: https://cryptoslate.com/tether-eyes-deeper-dive-into-gold-with-new-100-million-investment-amid-market-boom/

data.title: Omni announces rebranding to Nomina and initiates NOM token migration
data.description: According to the Omni Labs website, Omni has rebranded to Nomina and introduced a new name, logo, and visual identity. The company also announced a token migration, with OMNI being exchanged for NOM at a 1:75 ratio, with a circulating supply of 2.9 billion and a total supply of 7.5 billion. The new token contract address is 0x6e6F6d696e61decd6605bD4a57836c5DB6923340. Staking will not be available for NOM initially, and OMNI staking will be suspended upon launch. Staking and rewards will be automatically migrated one week later, without any user intervention.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation completed a partial sell-off and withdrew 3.387 million DAI from CEX
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember's monitoring, the Ethereum Foundation transferred 10,000 ETH to Kraken for sale the day before yesterday, and withdrew 3.387 million DAI from Kraken 20 minutes ago, and part of the ETH has been sold.

data.title: Sora Ventures Plans $1 Billion Fund for Bitcoin Reserve Companies in Asia
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Sora Ventures has announced its intention to establish a $1 billion fund aimed at investing in Bitcoin reserve companies across Asia. The venture capital firm revealed in a press release on Friday that it plans to raise the full amount within the next six months. Currently, Sora Ventures has secured an initial commitment of $200 million from regional partners and investors.

The fund will focus on companies headquartered in Asia that hold Bitcoin on their balance sheets. This investment strategy has been gaining traction among publicly listed companies in Japan, Hong Kong, Thailand, and South Korea in recent years.

data.title: The National Natural Science Foundation of China has released the application guide for the 2025 Stablecoin Risk Governance Research Project.
data.description: The Management Science Department of the National Natural Science Foundation of China recently released application guidelines for the third emergency management project in 2025, titled "Research on Global Stablecoin Risk Governance and Cross-Border Collaborative Regulatory Systems." This project focuses on the challenges posed by stablecoins to the international monetary system and their risk transmission mechanisms. It comprises one overarching project and five sub-projects, covering the structural evolution of stablecoins, their inherent vulnerabilities, their impact on monetary sovereignty, the impact on the financial intermediary system, and the global regulatory framework.

data.title: Justin Sun transferred $9 million worth of unlocked WLFI into HTX
data.description: According to Bubblemaps, Justin Sun transferred $9 million in unlocked WLFI to HTX. Over the past three days, he has transferred a total of $10 million to centralized exchanges. Earlier news broke that World Liberty Financial (WLFI) had blacklisted addresses associated with Justin Sun, implicating approximately 540 million unlocked tokens and 2.4 billion locked tokens. The project stated that it suspected exchanges were using user tokens for sell-offs, thereby depressing the token's price.

data.title: South Korea Caps Crypto Lending at 20% Interest, Bans Over-Collateralized Loans
data.description:
South Korea's Financial Services Commission implemented comprehensive crypto lending guidelines capping interest at 20% annually and banning over-collateralized loans after 27,600 investors borrowed 1. 5 trillion won ($1. 1 billion) in one month, with 13% facing forced liquidation due to market volatility.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/south-korea-caps-crypto-lending-at-20-interest-bans-over-collateralized-loans/
